conditioning: TAM prefix sits in the verb's slot after the subject marker (SM-TAM-root); na-/me- have habitual/stative alternants (hu-, -a)

examples: future ta- is sparse in the corpus
fit_none.note: the English pivot conflates perfect/past, so me- (perfect) projects to Past; future ta- is under-attested — the expected residue

evidence_citations.stat: label_tam derives na→Pres, li→Past, me→Past, ka→Past at confidence 1.0

prose: swh marks tense/aspect with a verb PREFIX in the slot after the subject marker: na- present, li- past, ta- future, me- perfect, ka- consecutive. The detector recovers na-/li-/me-/ka- from data (projected English Tense, confidence 1.0); future ta- is sparse in this corpus and the English pivot conflates perfect with past (me- → Past). Recommendation: PROMOTE na/li/me/ka; flag ta- (future) as data-sparse and me- as perfect-not-past (a known pivot conflation).
